schema.title: theory_generation

auto_discovery.mission: Source a cohort from the literature and run a fresh AutoDiscovery against a research question, then replicate each surprising candidate hypothesis on a held-out subset. Run as its own session in a separate workspace (own mission.md and .beads, since a second epic root in one workspace breaks epic-root.sh); the research question (the intent) comes from that mission.md. The intent and the cohort are the most important inputs to a good discovery run, so most of the work is front-loaded into cohort_assembly. This flow is distinct from `reproduction`, which imports an existing run rather than standing up a new one.

nodes.mission: Gather and cohort the data for discovery. Find the relevant papers, extract the numbers and the datasets they used, then source a cohort by fixing inclusion/exclusion and sampling, and hold an independent subset back for replication. Validate the assembled data against its source papers (n, schema/variables, units, missingness); a dataset that fails validation is a gap, not an input. Stand up and upload the discovery run (autodiscovery create, upload, metadata). Emit the cohort, with its discovery_subset, its held-out holdout_subset, and the stood-up run_id, alongside the registered datasets.
nodes.name: cohort_assembly

nodes.mission: Run discovery against the original question with the cohort as data (config n_experiments, set in the run metadata). Fetch the experiments; select the candidate hypotheses one-to-one from the log — every experiment whose `surprising` field is true — and emit them as autods_hypothesis identity records with the `hypothesis` field copied VERBATIM and the `experiment_plan`, `code`, and run signals (`surprising`, `posterior.mean`, `belief_change`, `normalized_surprisal`) carried unchanged, alongside the raw experiments. No grouping, merging, or rewording; the surprising nodes are the hypotheses, one per node.
nodes.name: discovery_run

nodes.mission: One branch per candidate hypothesis (created at replan, once discovery_run has named them). Replicate that hypothesis independently on the held-out subset.
nodes.name: replication

nodes.mission: Replicate the hypothesis on the held-out subset, one DataVoyager run per hypothesis, in parallel (at most config max_parallel_dv_runs concurrent submissions). Prepare and curate the held-out data with local tools as needed, but the replication experiment is executed by DataVoyager and its computed output is the result — do not run the test locally and report that as the replication. Instruct DataVoyager to execute the analysis in its own code environment and return the actual computed numbers and a figure; a reply that only returns code to run, asks you to run something, or ends without computed values is not a result — resubmit with a more directive prompt (up to config dv_max_attempts) until it returns computed results. The verdict comes from this replication, not from the discovery run, so emit an audit_report referencing the hypothesis id. Emit at least one figure behind the numbers. DataVoyager returns figures as imageb64, so decode each to a PNG under .asta/analyze-data/<slug>/figures/, put the path in figure.image (or render your own), and list them in `figures` with captions.
nodes.name: holdout_replication

nodes.mission: Summarize a completed research session as a self-contained encyclopedia entry. It composes onto any flow, reads the whole session, and names no inputs, so it runs last by id order.
nodes.name: summarize

nodes.mission: From the completed session's report, write a stand-alone encyclopedia entry a reader can understand without the original report. Give it a short, informative title and the index fields that place it in the encyclopedia. Write a context passage that orients the reader to the technical domain, setting the scientific context only, and do not repeat the research or the discovery. Then write an executive summary for each audience that states the finding succinctly and elaborates in a paragraph highlighting what is most novel or surprising versus what is already known. Write one for an intelligent layperson outside the field (simplify, avoid jargon) and one for a scientist working in the field, each standing on its own. Write the context, main finding, and elaboration as statements. When a statement rests on an AutoDiscovery run, a Theorizer artifact, or a DataVoyager run from this session, cite it by that run or artifact's own id copied from the session; never invent an id or cite one you cannot find in the session, and leave basic background knowledge uncited. Author output_json only; compile-report.py renders the cover, the deep-link header, and the body.
nodes.name: summarize

nodes.mission: Capture the executing agent's feedback on how the session ran. It composes onto any flow, reads the whole session, and names no inputs, so it runs last by id order.
nodes.name: reflection

nodes.mission: The session is finished. Look back over the run and record how it went, as feedback rather than science. Note where the flow, schema, or tools got in your way or cost you rework, and the concrete changes that would have made the run go better. Point at the real tasks, fields, and tools involved.
nodes.name: reflection

data_and_literature_grounded_theory_gene.mission: Reproduce an existing AutoDiscovery run's hypotheses on independent data, then generate literature-grounded theories from them. First obtain the run's experiments (auto_discovery); then, in parallel, gather the independent secondary data (evidence_gathering — provided in the mission or found by an open search) and run the thematic + intent literature searches (literature_search); fan back in and reproduce each selected hypothesis on the secondary data (reproduction branches); then theorize, summarize, and reflect. There is no data_provenance step and no recovery of the run's original dataset — only independent data matters.

nodes.mission: Execute AutoDiscovery, or — when the mission already provides a run directory — just load its experiment log (the mcts_node_*.json nodes) and run no fresh discovery. This step only obtains the experiments and makes a signal-based selection; it adds no scientific judgment. SELECT the candidate hypotheses ONE-TO-ONE from the log — no grouping, clustering, merging, splitting, or rewording: take every experiment whose `surprising` field is true, plus the config theorizer_top_n experiments with the highest `posterior.mean` and the theorizer_top_n with the lowest, deduplicated by node id. Each selected experiment becomes exactly one candidate hypothesis: copy its `hypothesis` field VERBATIM as the statement (never reword — a single changed word can invalidate it) and carry its `experiment_plan`, `code`, and run signals (`surprising`, `posterior.mean`, `belief_change`, `normalized_surprisal`) unchanged, so experiment_design can hand the same experiment to DataVoyager. Record each one's source_outcome from how its belief shifted (posterior.mean vs prior.mean). Keep the raw experiment nodes as artifacts.
nodes.name: auto_discovery

nodes.mission: Ground the theories in the literature, in parallel with evidence_gathering. Cluster the AutoDiscovery hypotheses into up to config n_thematic_searches themes and add one further theme that is the run's overall intent (from the run metadata); this phase fans out one branch per theme.
nodes.name: literature_search

nodes.mission: Search the literature for this one theme (a cluster of AutoDiscovery hypotheses, or the run's overall intent). Retrieve papers with the `asta-papers` PyPI package and, with the user's consent, computer-use for papers behind access friction. Emit a literature_review for the theme: key findings with evidence uuids, open gaps, and citations.
nodes.name: thematic_search

nodes.mission: Get the independent (secondary) data the hypotheses are reproduced on. If the mission provides or names a secondary dataset (a folder, product, or link), register that as the independent data and do not search further — the original AutoDiscovery dataset is not needed. Otherwise run ONE open-ended search across all hypotheses for independent datasets: independent means a different instrument, inventory, region, or method than the source; prefer the lightest product that carries the needed variables. When a hypothesis is a difference or ratio of variables, keep every term of that construct on the SAME source and datum — swapping one term to an independent product with a different vertical datum, grid, or units silently corrupts the construct and manufactures false nulls. Vary one independence axis at a time and record which axis each dataset changes. Exhaust the openly available options — repositories, products the source cites, lighter per-entity or subset extracts, APIs, mirrors, and data reachable only through browser automation — before deciding a hypothesis cannot be tested; a dataset being large or awkward to obtain is never a reason to stop. Validate each acquired dataset against its source before registering it; one that fails validation is a gap, not an input. Register which hypotheses each dataset can test (covers_hypotheses), and for every hypothesis left uncovered record a gap with the avenues actually tried, reserving gaps for quantities that are genuinely non-public or have no independent measurement. Every hypothesis appears in a dataset's covers_hypotheses or in gaps.
nodes.name: evidence_gathering

nodes.mission: One branch per selected hypothesis (created at replan, once auto_discovery has selected the hypothesis set). Reproduce that hypothesis on the independent data; the two-axis verdict (outcome crossed with testability) comes from the branch's audit, not the ingested run.
nodes.name: reproduction

nodes.mission: Adapt the AutoDiscovery experiment for the independent data — do not design a new one. Start from the hypothesis's carried AutoDS record (its verbatim `hypothesis`, `experiment_plan`, and `code`) and re-express the SAME test on the secondary dataset, changing only what the new data requires (column names, units, the load step); keep the statistic, the direction, and the decision the same as the AutoDS inner loop so autods and DataVoyager stay identical. State the original operationalization, the independent one, and whether they are equivalent or only a proxy. Hold the datum, grid, and units of every term of a difference or ratio construct consistent (do not mix elevation or thickness sources within one derived quantity), and vary a single independence axis per test so a non-reproduction is attributable to that axis rather than to a datum mismatch. Before committing, check whether the source hypothesis is an empirical relationship or an artifact of how the dataset was built, since a quantity defined from others is true by construction — a construct_mismatch to flag rather than a result to reproduce. Set feasibility from whether evidence_gathering has a secondary dataset that can test this hypothesis; no secondary dataset means feasibility data_unavailable and no DataVoyager run. Commit the prespecified test (test, metric, success_threshold) before any analysis runs. What happens next is plan's Gate, not this step's job. Feasible and proxy_only branches get analysis and audit; data_unavailable and construct_mismatch branches get only audit, wired to this design alone, recording outcome n/a and testability untestable.
nodes.name: experiment_design

nodes.mission: Run the reproduction on the acquired data, per the design's prespecified test. Using local tools to find, curate, validate, and reshape the data you send to DataVoyager is expected — merging products, deriving columns, fixing units, subsetting; what must NOT happen is running the reproduction test itself locally and reporting that as the result. The experiment is executed by DataVoyager and its computed output is the reproduction. Instruct DataVoyager to execute the analysis in its own code environment and return the actual computed numbers (effect size, p, n) plus at least one figure; a reply that only hands back code for you to run, asks you to run something, ends without computed values, or shuts down with no artifacts is not a result — resubmit with a more directive, imperative prompt (up to config dv_max_attempts) until DataVoyager returns computed results. A local computation may cross-check those numbers, but the reported effect size and outcome come from the DataVoyager run. Emit at least one figure behind the numbers. DataVoyager returns figures as imageb64, so decode each to a PNG under .asta/analyze-data/<slug>/figures/, put the path in figure.image (or render your own), and list them in `figures` with captions.
nodes.name: analysis

nodes.mission: Try to refute the analysis or find artifacts, then finalize the hypothesis's verdict. Beyond a negative control that reruns with the predictor shuffled (or equivalent) and confirms the effect disappears, probe sensitivity to influential observations and reasonable subset choices, and report when the verdict is sample-dependent rather than robust. Before finalizing a non-reproduction, rule out an analysis-setup artifact — confirm the independent operationalization preserved the construct's datum, grid, and units and varied only the intended independence axis, and re-test with source-matched auxiliary variables where a mismatch is possible; a null that appears only under a mismatched datum or a single confounded substitution is a setup artifact to fix and re-run, not a failed reproduction. Then finalize the hypothesis's two-axis verdict (outcome crossed with testability), independence axes, and observed effect size from the analysis, or from the experiment_design alone as outcome n/a and testability untestable when the branch was infeasible, checked against the design's prespecified success_threshold and judged by effect size rather than statistical significance alone on large samples. Also emit the reproduction signal (reward and basis), the signal the theorizer reads. When the hypothesis reproduces, give a positive reward with basis reproduced. When the effect does not replicate on the secondary data, give a negative reward with basis not_reproduced, and keep the hypothesis, since this is a negative signal not a removal. When you are confident the source finding is factually wrong (an AutoDS mistake), set reward -1 with basis refuted_incorrect, which drops it from the theorizer. When there is no secondary dataset and DataVoyager never ran, give no reward with basis not_evaluated. Record in source_agreement whether this independent verdict agrees with or contradicts the hypothesis's own source_outcome, flagging a contradiction prominently. Emit an audit_report referencing the hypothesis id. The hypothesis record itself is never re-emitted.
nodes.name: audit

nodes.mission: Explain the cross-cutting theories behind the reproduced hypotheses — anchored to the run's own stated intent and dataset context, grounded in the literature and the reproduction's numbers — by fanning out one Theorizer run per generation objective, then triaging the theories for testability. Novelty is read from the Theorizer's own per-statement novelty_evaluation, not scored separately at the theory level.
nodes.name: theorizer

nodes.mission: Fan out one branch per generation objective — accuracy_focused and novelty_focused — so each objective is its own Theorizer run (n = 2 branches).
nodes.name: theory_formation

nodes.mission: Run the managed Theorizer once for THIS branch's generation objective. Reconstruct the original user's intent from the run's metadata — its stated intent and dataset descriptions — and distil it into the query and framing, so the theories serve what the user set out to learn. On the reproduced hypotheses, generate theories under this objective (accuracy_focused: grounded in established results; novelty_focused: non-obvious mechanisms and new predictions), each tagged with the objective, all relevant to the user's intent and cross-cutting (each explaining several reproduced hypotheses), grounded in both the literature and the reproduction's effect sizes and verdicts. Preserve each theory's per-statement novelty_evaluation verbatim in its theory_components — that statement-level novelty is what the report surfaces. Drop any hypothesis the audit confirmed as a mistake; keep a hypothesis that did not reproduce but carry its negative signal as conflicting evidence. Populate conflicting_evidence, and make unaccounted_for address the partial and untestable hypotheses.
nodes.name: theory_generation

nodes.mission: Triage each theory for testability, recording the decision as a signal, by comparing its required data against the independent data in hand (whatever evidence_gathering provided or found). A theory is testable now only if its prediction is empirically free in that data; a prediction that is definitionally entailed by how those variables were constructed, or that requires transient or time-resolved data, is not testable now even when the variables are present. For each testable theory, commit a prespecified proposed_test it could be checked against; theories needing new data are recorded untestable. Novelty is not re-scored here at the theory level — carry the Theorizer's per-statement novelty_evaluation through — and list the testable theory ids.
nodes.name: novelty_assessment

nodes.name: summarize

Write one for an intelligent layperson outside the field (simplify, avoid jargon) and one for a scientist working in the field, each standing on its own. Write the context, main finding, and elaboration as statements. When a statement rests on an AutoDiscovery run, a Theorizer artifact, or a DataVoyager run from this session, cite it by that run or artifact's own id copied from the session; never invent an id or cite one you cannot find in the session, and leave basic background knowledge uncited. Author output_json only; compile-report.py renders the cover, the deep-link header, and the body.
nodes.name: summarize

nodes.name: reflection

nodes.name: reflection
